I have got to the stage that I'm hesitating going to see my own parents encase i catch somethingDebbie 
Thank you for your comment. 
Sunnymeade like all Aged care providers are obliged in accordance with legislation, regulations, standards, and protocols.to follow the protocols set out by the State government and Metro North Healthcare.
 Metro North outbreak procedure is defined as two or more linked cases of the same illness or the situation where the observed number of cases exceeds the expected number, or a single case of disease caused by a significant pathogen.
Debbie outbreak control measures are acts which attempt to minimize the spread of or effects of a disease outbreak for all our residents well being.
This is a precautionary measure to help minimise the risk to our elderly residents. These alerts do not mean that Sunnymeade has more or less of any community illnesses going around. It just means Sunnymeade want everyone to be kept updated.
Please feel free to contact me for any more information.
Regards
Anthony

reviewDetails.text: Nice village great residents lots to do but management leaves a lot to be desired.Nice village great residents lots to do but management leaves a lot to be desired.
I didn't go there.I didn't go there.

Green Wattle Sanctuary is A designated village for people over age 50. This is an "over 50's mobile home park". The houses are owned, the site is leased. In Australia OVER 50's VILLAGES have regulations set by the Australian Government to protect some interests of the house and company owners. This is a major difference from retirement villages.
Green Wattle Sanctuary is a beautifully laid out place in well kept gardens and lawns. As owners of the dwellings, not the land, residents have many very nice, different houses. They are quite free to decorate and detail their houses to suit their tastes. The Sanctuary has a beautiful swimming pool, tennis court and shared recreational hall. That is used for functions, meetings, private group activities, games and more. On a Friday night residents can choose to have a community dinner in the hall for just $5.
The roads in Sanctary are very good indeed and have a 10km speed limit. They are shared pedestrian, vehicle. As the area is by a small national park (koala sanctuary) there is an abundant bird life in and around GWS. It's quite evident and very nice. Crows, Magpies, Kookaburras ocassionally, Parrots, Doves, and many more. There's a good number of trees, shrubs and flowers to support them too.
Buses that circle a wide area are pretty much at the gates. Shopping centre in Burpengary is only 3 mins by car. It's a well established place with some owners/residents having lived here for 20+ years. It has the very best community hall of any place I ever saw. Huge, new purpose built building freely available to residents for many activities. e.g. Friday nights there is a meal available for $5 per person, provisioned by a voluntary group of owners. A large bowling green with bowls provided for residents and their visitors. Much more too. It's generally a quiet place with the low hum of traffic from the nearby Bruce highway. I would like to commend you and your staff at Sunnymeade Park for the quality care afforded to my grand mother Vera  during her time with you. On my visits to see her I was always impressed with the cleansliness, the attitude of the staff and the conditions and standards. My mother has told me how Mrs Walker the owner has shown compassion and this is also reflected in the actions of all the staff. I thank you also for your assistance to my family.
I am a nurse myself and have always been extremely impressed by the standards I have seen when visiting, The homely atmosphere and the attention given to the residents. I cannot fault the care and I know Vera (Nan) was happy, comfortable and loved in your care.
I would like to thank all the staff who were involved in Nans care at the end of her life, particularly to the Nurse/Carer who made the effort to pick some roses for nan on her passing to be place with her. This action was very touching and was comforting to my mother who was present. Please pass on my heartfelt thanks to all your staff at all levels.

Be careful.
This is really a high dependency dementia facility when residents can foam freely within the facility and into any accommodation they wish.
My dad as a non-dimentia resident found it very mentally challenging as those who caused the most disturbance got the attention of carers, meaning dad was essentially ignored.
Both he and my self were verbally abused and he was physically assaulted by a fellow resident.
Whilst all incidents were reported in writing, the responses were hollow and meaningless.
I was lucky enough to find a placement in a facility that is split between dementia and cognitive people and in a very short time is back to his old self.
I'm only sorry NDC was not sold to me as it actually is, and even more sorry I put my folks through that torture for those 5 months.

We are so disappointed in ourselves for placing Mum here. Most of the permanent staff are awesome, caring people but  often there are relief staff who have no commitment. Most often there are insufficient staff on hand to deal with the needs of the residents during busy times.
After 3 weeks lockdown I found my Mum distressed, fingernails long and broken (cutting her skin), no toothbrush (don’t know how long her teeth weren’t cleaned. Staff walking around with masks below their nose, visitors walking in and out unchecked for temperature, masks.
We CONSTANTLY have to follow up basic care needs which are addressed immediately by permanent caring staff. But the same issues reappear regularly. A family member visits daily to ensure her needs are met. Weekends find the front reception unattended and minimum staff on hand allowing visitors to enter on keypad entry. This also allows for residents to slip out the door unnoticed. Very disappointed.
2023 now Into our 3rd year there now and nothing has improved.  Staff are constantly rotating through key roles which means that every time that happens, Mums care is affected.  Constantly attending meetings where promises are made but not followed up. It was absolutely heartbreaking for me to acknowledge that I could no longer care for my darling husband at home. I checked out nine different facilities and TriCare Labrador was and is my first choice.  Fortunately I eventually got a room there for Alan. He adapted immediately and is well settled and content .  I and our family couldn't wish for a more caring, homely and comfortable place for him to see out his days.  The staff are always welcoming no matter what time of day any of us pop in and an invitation is often extended to join in a cup of tea, lunch  or whatever is happening at the time of visit.  The food is excellent, well presented and with good variety and even accompanied by a glass or wine or beer for the residents if desired !
Alan's rapidly progressing Alzheimer's make his care challenging and beyond my capabilities - the TriCare Labrador staff treat him with the utmost care and respect at all times.  They jolly him along  to elicit the occasional smile  and also encourage Alan to participate in the various
occupational activities and exercise schedule.
A HUGE Thank You to all of the TriCare Team for providing the stellar care and service that you do and for allowing me peace of mind in the knowledge that Alan is receiving the best care possible

It doesn't even deserve one but there was no other option. The food was so terrible my Labrador sniffed it and walked away. My mother rang the bell for assistance to go to  the the bathroom, it either was answered two hours later or not at all. Her drawers were crawling with hundreds of cockroaches, dead and alive. I alerted staff and asked for some pest control substance. They replied they couldn't access any. When my mum came out of hospital and returned to her room at Tricare, she needed to go to the toilet. The Staff  left her alone and she blacked out from an horrific fall. They later allowed her to fall from her chair, her skull was pierced by a sharp object. THEY NEVER EXPLAINED WHAT HAPPENED despite numerous requests. . She died several days later. Then, they charged us an extra 15K for the EXTRA care they gave. They refused to settle the financial contract UNLESS WE PAID FIFTEEN THOUSAND DOLLARS FOR THE WORST NIGHTMARE OF HER LIFE. She was a dignified human being who had dedicated her life to the betterment and service of others. Tricare Labrador should be shut down. She wasn't allowed fruit juice. The kitchen staff told me they were forced to serve cheapnasty cordial unless doctor ordered juice due to constipation. I was told the total allowance per resident for food was $2 per day? The WORST and most telling proof of neglect was massive deep bedsores that they used to tell us were dressed but on inspection were NOT. My mother was in such agony the doctor had to give her morphine patches and Fentanol. I could fill a journal with these tales of woe. The air-conditioned remotes used to be hidden and the patients sweltered in the oppressive HEAT. The only food Mum ate was what we brought in. One of the kind assistants used to buy fruit and give it to Mum because she felt so sorry for her. The only RN overseeing the whole place on weekends did not have the requisite standard of English. Complaints were answered with stock phrases, blank expressions and promises that never eventuated. Mostly, we waited at the empty desk and no-one seemed to be on duty. At least, we couldn't raise them. My many regrets are exacerbated by my failure to send a report to the Parliamentary Enquiry. I didn't have Power of Attorney so had no authority to choose or change the facility.
I read your reply. I am willing to swear in court about what I wrote. I spoke to the kitchen cook MYSELF. She said, unless Mum had bowel trouble diagnosed by the Dr, she was NOT allowed juice, ONLY CORDIAL. There were TWO staff who witnessed the hundreds of cockcroaches. THEY were the ones who told me the treatment was upstairs, locked in a cupboard. I can SHOW you where the aircon remotes were kept. NO patient could reach them.. My mother HAD BRUISES on her head and body from falls and the final one killed her. I WROTE to TRICARE with PHOTOS of white bread sandwiches with minimal filling. Almost none. I received AN APOLOGY and a promise to improve. I was employed at a University for fifty years, working with languages. I recognise the ability or otherwise re command of English. Our family could NOT make ourselves understood to the ONLY R. N on duty on weekends.

This went on for about 10 minutes, it was obvious that someone has fallen or was …Hi Tamara, 

Thank you for your concern regarding our resident. We pride ourselves on making Kewarra Aged Care a home for our residents. It is not a facility, and it is not an institution. Our residents can freely come and go as they like into the local community. They are also able to freely come and go as they like into the gardens and courtyards that are part of the home.

I can confirm that one resident did have a fall in the garden and that our team were alerted by a neighbour, for which we are thankful.

The resident was located and quickly attended to by a GP who was at the home conducting scheduled appointments with other residents. The resident sustained no injuries and is doing well. As a precaution the resident was visited the following day by her own GP and no concerns were raised.

The family of the resident has been kept informed at every point and remain thankful and appreciative of the care that we provide. The daughter of our resident has read your comment and does not wish to pursue as she is supportive of the actions taken. They know our local team well as their family member has been living at the home for the past five years.

As required, the home reported the incident to the various authorities.

Tamara, once again thank you for raising this concern.

I could not recommend Steve and the Right at Home team in Cairns more highly! When my Mum got sick and we needed to provide her with care at home I was very keen to make sure we provided her with the very best quality care - with people you could trust and rely on.

Right at Home came recommended to us, and they could not have been more spot on - from the very beginning Steve and the team treated my mum and all of the family with the upmost dignity, respect and care. You could tell they GENUINELY cared about the welfare of my mum and wanting to get the best outcome for her.

The whole process of dealing with them from the beginning was easy and you felt like YOU were their priority and the service and quality of care Mum received was excellent. One of their nurses just won a national award and they hand pick the very best of staff.

They were always willing to help out when we needed them and provided a reliable consistent source of support and care when we needed it most. Steve is EXTREMELY knowledgeable about all things Home Care and will help you navigate through the complex and confusing processes with ease. Steve was a breath of fresh air amidst all the confusion and noise and helped me make sense of it all!!

At such a difficult time for our family they were like a trusted friend alongside you for the ride, offering help and support. They stand apart from all the others in the industry and the other home care providers offering services in Cairns (we have tried a few before Right at home).

As I said, give them a call - you will not be disappointed and you will get the reliable, high quality care in place that you desire and deserve for your loved ones. They are setting the industry standard here that all other providers should follow.

Do not send your family here! They have zero care factor and you are only another number to them. My grandma had the worst care while she was there for 4 months before passing in 2021. She ended up passing away from a clot in her foot, which took almost 3 days for the staff to respond too. When she complained of pain in her foot, they said she was just upset about her family leaving and wasn’t actually in pain. Meanwhile she had no blood flow to her foot and it was slowly and painfully dying. Eventually they called an ambulance in the middle of the night. They also sent the hospital medical documents and information for another lady, instead of hers. Prior to this, the response for cleaning her up and getting her ready for bed would be delayed by hours and she would be left with burning on her behind skin and trying to sleep sitting up. The way they spoke to her was appalling! I met one nice worker who seemed to care, the rest had no care factor! When we went in to collect her things after she passed away in the hospital, we told the staff upon entry that she had passed and she literally replied with “oh ok”.
We were in the process of moving her before she passed.. do your loved ones a favour and don’t send them here in the first place!!Do not send your family here! Whilst placing a loved one in care can be emotional and distressing, the team at Caloundra Place Care Community set us at ease from our first walk through the door.  They could not have been more kind, professional or welcoming to our Mum.  Since placement we have watched our mother thrive in every way, medically, nutritionally, socially; she has made many new friends amongst staff and fellow clients and loves being part of the family atmosphere.
